data.title: Netflix shakes things up once more with Warner Bros. deal. Their shift towards theatrical releases is their most surprising move yet.
data.description: The popular streaming service has altered its approach numerous times in the past. However, the recent move to heavily invest in theatrical releases appears to be the most surprising one yet.

data.title: WiresConnect announces second conference on December 10, 2025, in NY featuring Deborah Weinswig and Daymond John.
data.description: In New York City, WiresConnect, founded by Kimberly Carney, CEO & Founder of The Wires, is set to host its second exclusive conference on December 10, 2025, at the Virgin Hotel. This invite-only event brings together visionary designers, entrepreneurs, and industry leaders in fashion and beauty to shape the future. Featuring curated panels and networking opportunities, the conference promises to build on the success of its inaugural event, with high attendance expected.

data.title: Cerebras AI Inference wins Demo of the Year at TSMC North America Technology Symposium in Sunnyvale.
data.description: A company based in Sunnyvale, California was recently recognized for its AI inference technology. Cerebras received the Demo of the Year Award at the TSMC North America Technology Symposium. This prestigious award highlights the innovative capabilities of Cerebras in the field of artificial intelligence. It signifies the company's exceptional performance and leadership in AI technology. The recognition at such a renowned event like the TSMC Symposium further solidifies Cerebras' position in the industry. This achievement showcases Cerebras' commitment to advancing AI technology and its significant impact on the market.

data.title: Simplify Group forms as parent company for Simplify Healthcare and SimplifyX™, emphasizing innovation and leadership appointments.
data.description: Simplify Group, a technology holding company based in Aurora, Illinois, has been established as the overarching entity for Simplify Healthcare and SimplifyX™. This marks a significant development in the company's journey, emphasizing a consolidated framework to foster advancement and expansion within its array of healthcare payer and non-healthcare offerings. Mohammed Vaid, the founder of the company, has shifted from the position of CEO to Founder in light of this transition.

data.title: S&P 500 near record, up 0.2% to 6,875, tech stocks climb 0.4%, inflation drops to 2.8%. Fed likely to cut rates next week.
data.description: By midday on Friday, major Wall Street indexes saw a slight increase, driven by promising economic data that raised expectations for a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in the upcoming week. The S&P 500 rose by 0.2% to approximately 6,875, still trailing its late‑October peak by about half a percentage point. Tech stocks also saw a 0.4% increase, although they remained almost 2 percentage points lower than their previous highs. In September, the core Personal Consumption Expenditures price index, which is the Fed's preferred measure of inflation, dipped from 2.9% to 2.8% year over year, slightly below predictions. The CME FedWatch tool indicates an 87% probability of a 25‑basis‑point cut in the following week. The University of Michigan's preliminary December consumer survey displayed an upturn in overall sentiment from 51 to 53.3, along with further softening of inflation expectations. Among mega‑cap stocks, Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) saw a notable surge of over 5%, aiming for a sixth consecutive day of gains subsequent to a decline of more than 50% from its January 2024 highs.

data.title: Venezuela has the world's largest oil reserves, but faces challenges in production amid tensions with the US.
data.description: With the Trump administration, tensions are rising in a South American nation boasting the largest oil reserves globally.

data.title: Bitcoin may switch to a 2-year cycle due to institutional flows and ETFs, impacting 2026 significantly - Jeff Park from ProCap BTC discloses.
data.description: Jeff Park from ProCap BTC discusses the potential impact of institutional flows and ETFs on Bitcoin's market cycle. He suggests that these factors could lead to a shorter cycle for Bitcoin, resulting in significant implications as we approach 2026.
